title: lessingia occidentalis
intro: Lessingia occidentalis, also known as Western Coyote Mint, is a perennial herb native to the coastal regions of California and Oregon in the United States. It belongs to the Asteraceae family and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to make herbal preparations that can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, and also provide relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach discomfort. Herbal preparations of Lessingia occidentalis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
